The Interaction Device table provides a detailed view of the devices associated with a consumer’s interactions within the DCE2.0 ecosystem. It links each interaction to the device or devices used, enabling a clearer understanding of device involvement in consumer activities such as onboarding, troubleshooting, lending, replacement, or product usage. This table complements the main Interactions dataset by offering enriched context on how specific devices support or influence consumer engagements.

values: Unique original identifier of the user (identity_unique_identifier)
Unique ID of the interaction. Reference data: Action_ID (interaction_id)

The brand family, e.g. Marlboro or IQOS, is a unique and distinct identity that represents PMI and its product and/or services. It includes logo, visual identity, messaging, values and typically have a trademark that protects this asset. (devices_brand_family_name)
2 letters ISO country code; Reference Data: Country (country_refcode)
Code identifier of the device type (devices_device_description)
Version of the asset. Reference data: Device Version (devices_device_version)
It indicates from when the record is Active

The Interaction Flavour table captures information about the specific flavour used by a consumer during an interaction. By enriching interaction records with product‑specific flavour details, this table supports analysis of consumer preferences, product usage patterns, and market‑level flavour trends.

values: Code of the flavour of the Heets used during the interaction (flavours_flavour_refcode)
Unique original identifier of the user (identity_unique_identifier)
Unique ID of the interaction. Reference data: Action_ID (interaction_id)
2 letters ISO country code; Reference Data: Country (country_refcode)
Name of the flavour of the Heets used during the interaction (flavours_flavour_refname)
It indicates from when the record is Active

The Interaction Product table records information about the specific assets or products associated with a consumer’s interaction. By linking interactions to the related assets, this table supports analysis of product usage, asset involvement in service or commercial activities, and market‑level product performance.

values: Unique original identifier of the user (identity_unique_identifier)
Unique ID of the interaction. Reference data: Action_ID (interaction_id)
Identifier of the assets related with the interaction (derived; can be used as Product Code available in TD-based PBI dataflows) (products_product_id)
2 letters ISO country code; Reference Data: Country (country_refcode)
Description of the assets related with the interaction (products_product_name)
It indicates from when the record is Active

Pipeline name: ${job_name}

Layer: PRESENTATION

Environment: ${ev_default_system_env}

Message:

Harmonization Pipeline fails - Please check the result!

The Interaction Voucher table captures information about vouchers used by consumers during checkout or other service‑related activities. It records voucher identifiers and associated details for each interaction, enabling visibility into how vouchers influenced the transaction, including any price reductions applied to the consumer’s purchase. This dataset supports the analysis of voucher utilization, promotional effectiveness, and consumer behavior related to discounts and incentive programs.

values: Unique original identifier of the user (identity_unique_identifier)
Unique ID of the interaction. Reference data: Action_ID (interaction_id)
Identifier of the voucher if used during the interaction (vouchers_voucher_id)
2 letters ISO country code; Reference Data: Country (country_refcode)
Amount of the voucher used in the interaction (vouchers_voucher_amount)
Name of the voucher related with the interaction. If any voucher was used: Null (vouchers_voucher_name)
It indicates from when the record is Active

1.value:
-- Makes sure each BK only have 1 active flag = true
WITH VERSIONS_CNT AS (
    SELECT 
        IDENTITY_UNIQUE_IDENTIFIER_BK, INTERACTION_IDENTIFIER_BK, DEVICE_IDENTIFIER_BK,
        COUNT(*) AS total_records,
        SUM(CASE WHEN ACTIVE_FLAG = TRUE THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS ACTIVE_FLAG_CNT
    FROM ${ev_database_30_presentation}.PL_INTERACTION."MDE_CONSUMER_INTERACTION_DEVICE"
    GROUP BY IDENTITY_UNIQUE_IDENTIFIER_BK, INTERACTION_IDENTIFIER_BK, DEVICE_IDENTIFIER_BK
)
SELECT 'MDE_CONSUMER_INTERACTION_DEVICE: distinct BK cnt comparison' AS TEST_TYPE, s.CNT AS SOURCE, t.CNT AS TARGET
FROM (
    SELECT COUNT(*) AS CNT
    FROM VERSIONS_CNT
) as s
CROSS JOIN (
    SELECT COUNT(*) AS CNT
    FROM VERSIONS_CNT
    WHERE ACTIVE_FLAG_CNT = 1
) as t

UNION ALL

-- Makes sure there is no record that has VALID_FROM >= VALID_TO
SELECT 'MDE_CONSUMER_INTERACTION_DEVICE: VALID_FROM >= VALID_TO' AS TEST_TYPE, s.CNT AS SOURCE, t.CNT AS TARGET
FROM (
	SELECT COUNT(*) AS CNT
    FROM ${ev_database_30_presentation}.PL_INTERACTION."MDE_CONSUMER_INTERACTION_DEVICE"
) as s
CROSS JOIN (
    SELECT COUNT(*) AS CNT
    FROM ${ev_database_30_presentation}.PL_INTERACTION."MDE_CONSUMER_INTERACTION_DEVICE"
    WHERE VALID_FROM < VALID_TO
) as t

UNION ALL

-- Makes sure that the count distinct of the BK composite key in Staging == Presentation
SELECT 'MDE_CONSUMER_INTERACTION_DEVICE: distinct BK cnt comparison' AS TEST_TYPE, s.CNT AS SOURCE, t.CNT AS TARGET
FROM (
    SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT identity_unique_identifier, interaction_id, devices_device_id) AS CNT
    FROM ${ev_database_10_staging}."STG_DCE2"."T_DCE2_INTERACTION_DEVICE" i
    WHERE TRY_TO_TIMESTAMP_NTZ(i.date_time) <= i.__LOAD_TS OR TRY_TO_TIMESTAMP_NTZ(i.date_time) IS NULL
) s
CROSS JOIN (
    SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT IDENTITY_UNIQUE_IDENTIFIER_BK, INTERACTION_IDENTIFIER_BK, DEVICE_IDENTIFIER_BK) AS CNT
    FROM ${ev_database_30_presentation}.PL_INTERACTION."MDE_CONSUMER_INTERACTION_DEVICE"
) t

-- Makes sure each BK only have 1 active flag = true
WITH VERSIONS_CNT AS (
    SELECT 
        IDENTITY_UNIQUE_IDENTIFIER_BK, INTERACTION_IDENTIFIER_BK, FLAVOUR_REFERENCE_CODE_BK,

        SUM(CASE WHEN ACTIVE_FLAG = TRUE THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS ACTIVE_FLAG_CNT
    FROM ${ev_database_30_presentation}.PL_INTERACTION."MDE_CONSUMER_INTERACTION_FLAVOUR"
    GROUP BY IDENTITY_UNIQUE_IDENTIFIER_BK, INTERACTION_IDENTIFIER_BK, FLAVOUR_REFERENCE_CODE_BK
)
SELECT 'MDE_CONSUMER_INTERACTION_FLAVOUR: distinct BK cnt comparison' AS TEST_TYPE, s.CNT AS SOURCE, t.CNT AS TARGET
FROM (
    SELECT COUNT(*) AS CNT
    FROM VERSIONS_CNT
) as s
CROSS JOIN (
    SELECT COUNT(*) AS CNT
    FROM VERSIONS_CNT
    WHERE ACTIVE_FLAG_CNT = 1
) as t

UNION ALL

-- Makes sure there is no record that has VALID_FROM >= VALID_TO
SELECT 'MDE_CONSUMER_INTERACTION_FLAVOUR: VALID_FROM >= VALID_TO' AS TEST_TYPE, s.CNT AS SOURCE, t.CNT AS TARGET
FROM (
	SELECT COUNT(*) AS CNT
    FROM ${ev_database_30_presentation}.PL_INTERACTION."MDE_CONSUMER_INTERACTION_FLAVOUR"
    -- WHERE VALID_FROM < VALID_TO --qas only since there is FLAVOUR_REFERENCE_CODE_BK with null values
) as s
CROSS JOIN (
    SELECT COUNT(*) AS CNT
    FROM ${ev_database_30_presentation}.PL_INTERACTION."MDE_CONSUMER_INTERACTION_FLAVOUR"
    WHERE VALID_FROM < VALID_TO
) as t

UNION ALL

-- Makes sure that the count distinct of the BK composite key in Staging == Presentation
SELECT 'MDE_CONSUMER_INTERACTION_FLAVOUR: distinct BK cnt comparison' AS TEST_TYPE, s.CNT AS SOURCE, t.CNT AS TARGET

Send an Email with HTML Body type - TDE_CONSUMER_INTERACTION
Harmonization Pipeline Check has failed for table TDE_CONSUMER_INTERACTION in PRESENTATION layer!

Pipeline name: ${job_name}

Table/View name: TDE_CONSUMER_INTERACTION

Layer: PRESENTATION

History id: ${run_history_id}

Environment: ${ev_default_system_env}

Message:

Harmonization Pipeline fails - Please check the result!

The Interactions table provides a consolidated overview of consumer engagements across all channels in the DCE2.0 ecosystem. It captures the nature and context of each interaction, including the interaction type, the channel used, and when relevant, details about retailers, CSC agents, or coaches. This dataset enables a unified understanding of consumer touchpoints and supports consistent analysis of consumer experience and operational performance.

values: Unique original identifier of the user (identity_unique_identifier)
Unique ID of the interaction. Reference data: Action_ID (interaction_id)
Identifier of the CSC agent present in the interaction (agent_id)

Status if it is an age verification interaction
true/false boolean value (state_id)
Brand family name of the product that is used in the interaction (brand_family)
Type of the B2C Case. Reference data: Case Type. (case_type)
Specifies the channel in which the interaction occurred. Reference Data: Interaction Channel (channel_refcode)
Identifier of the IQOS Coach present in the interaction (coach_id)
Identifier of the Heets contract signed by consumers (contract_id)
Required to correlate DCE 2 data point with legacy system transaction (correlation_id)
Code of the currency that is used during a purchase interaction (currency)
Type of document used for the age verification (document_type)
Unique identifier of the retailer in DTE system (dte_retailer_id)
Identifier of the event related to the interaction (event_id)
External identifier for interactions happening in indirect retail (external_reference)
Indicates eligibility outcome of device diagnostic; populated for QURE interaction only, has Y/N value (eligible/not eligible) (final_result)
Unique identifier of the person (friend) that referred in case it happened (friend_id)
Reason for the interaction. Reference data: Reason (reason)
Description of the reason of the interaction (reason_description)
Identifier of the reason for the interaction (reason_id)
Reason of the interaction. Reference code: Interaction Reason (reason_refcode)

External identifier of the interaction in SAP system.
For Self Service Registrations ie IQoS Plus - the SAPID is used to identify the store - what the customer enters to identify the store (alternate ID), they will enter the SAP_ID as this is a more user-friendly id. (sap_id)
The unique identifier of the interaction session (session_id)
Type of interaction. Reference data: Interaction type (interaction_type_refcode)
Full adress (or part of it) of the location where the interaction occured (address of the retailer store) (location_address)
City of the address (address of the retailer store) (location_address_city)
2 letters ISO country code of the country from the location address  (location_address_country_refcode)
line_1 of the location address, can be street name, number and optional appartement number (address of the retailer store) (location_address_line_1)
Zip code of the location where the interaction took place (address of the retailer store) (location_address_zip_code)
Short description of the location where interaction takes place (location_description)
Identifier of the location in which the interaction took place (location_id)
Name of the location where the interaction occurred (location_name)
Type (shop, hotel, etc. ) of the location where interaction takes place (location_type_refcode)
Amount of the order if applicable (order_amount)
It\'s the ID through which the subscription has been identified by AD service (subscription_id)
Payment method of the order if applicable. Reference Data: Payment Scheme/ Brand (payment_method)
The type of the SFP platform that product belongs to if interactions includes a product (platform_brand)
Preferred platform for the consumer. Reference data: Platform Brand (preferred_platform)
Picked up from the product_catalog, values: ht-device/ ht-accessories/ ht-consumables / pv-device/ pv-accessories/ pv-consumables (category)

To link the RAAS retailer carrying out the Registration (Identity) to the related Interaction record.
SOURCE: FSL - Keys for Store Master - DCE 2.0 Home - PMI DCE Confluence (retailer_id)
Name of the retailer where the interaction occurred (retailer_name)
Shows which system/source has triggered the interaction. (app_id)
Name of the vendor that triggers the interaction (vendor)
It indicates from when the record is Active

    SELECT 
        IDENTITY_UNIQUE_IDENTIFIER_BK, INTERACTION_IDENTIFIER_BK,
        COUNT(*) AS total_records,

)
SELECT 'TDE_CONSUMER_INTERACTION: distinct BK cnt comparison' AS TEST_TYPE, s.CNT AS SOURCE, t.CNT AS TARGET
FROM (
    SELECT COUNT(*) AS CNT
    FROM VERSIONS_CNT

    SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT identity_unique_identifier, interaction_id) AS CNT
